kept.text:
EXCLUSIVE: @_TalkingTokens sat down with Max Stein, a director on @BlackRock's digital assets team for his first podcast interview.

We get an inside look at BlackRock's digital asset strategy, why tokenized cash will be the next huge opportunity, investor appetite for the industry and more.

TIMESTAMPS
00:00 – Intro
01:18 – How Max defines tokenization and his role at BlackRock
01:40 – How BlackRock goes from idea to product in digital assets
03:16 – Regulatory environment and how it affects the pace of product development
05:21 – Stablecoins at BlackRock: reserve management and internal use
06:17 – Why BlackRock chose @Securitize and how BUIDL was designed
07:54 – How the FTX collapse actually accelerated BlackRock's tokenization work
08:54 – Why tokenized cash is still the biggest opportunity
14:09 – The shift from private chains to public networks and the hybrid model
22:17 – Stablecoins as utility: programmable money, flash loans, and same-rail settlement
27:36 – His thoughts on DeFi exploits, contagion, and what it means for tokenized asset design
33:13 – How AI agents may prefer onchain rails and accelerate crypto adoption
38:50 – Final advice: small decisions shape whether a product scales or sits on a shelf

Full episode below:

kept.text:
Computershare (the transfer agent for 58% of the S&P 500) just partnered with @Securitize to put actual shares onchain.

Not derivative tokens or synthetic claims, but direct equity ownership in a wallet.

Computershare serves 25,000+ companies, handling dividends & stock splits - and now they'll do it across both traditional and tokenized formats simultaneously.

The TAM here is the entire $70T U.S. equity market.

Tokenized equities today are ~$1.1B - that's 63,000x from the full TAM.

article.title: RWAs: The sleeping giant in crypto
article.preview_text:
TradFi is officially entering crypto.
This isn’t speculation anymore, it’s already happening.
And here's the magnet that's drawing them in:
Real World Asset (RWA) Tokenization.
The idea is simple:

kept.text:
$CEPT / @Securitize IMO is the only real pure play on tokenization.

• $4.33B tokenized AUM already. 

• Powers @BlackRock $2.58B BUIDL fund.

• Hand-picked by the @NYSE to build their tokenized securities platform.

• Just announced a game changing partnership with @Computershare, the world’s largest transfer agent, to let thousands of U.S.-listed companies issue real tokenized shares alongside traditional stock.

• Revenue for the 9 months ended Sept. 30, 2025 came in at $55.6M, up 841% YoY vs. $5.9M in the same period a year earlier. Full year 2024 was just $18.8M, meaning they nearly tripled the whole prior year in three quarters.

• Management guided $16M EBITDA for all of 2025 (23% margin), but Securitize had already delivered $25M of EBITDA in just the first 9 months, at 45% margins, nearly double the projected rate.

• Investors but not limited to:  @ARKInvest @BlackRock @bcap @MorganStanley @Tradeweb @Aptos @AvaLabs @blockchain @circle @cbventures 

• Lockup is CLEAN. No warrants, 6 month lockup period post merger, and 100% of early investors are rolling equity. With downside(NAV) only at $10.43. Fully committed $225M pipe

Most SPAC targets of the last 5 years went public with zero revenue and projections built on vibes. Securitize is coming in already profitable, beat and raising, with blue-chip clients like BlackRock, Apollo, and Hamilton Lane already on the platform​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​.

Disclosure: Currently own 35K commons at $11.53.

kept.text:
I lost a sale.  

Earlier in my career, I represented a leading digital asset subledger product and submitted a proposal to an RFP that came in 10x higher than my nearest competitor's.  

The CFO called me. Still wanted to go with us because they understood the value we brought to the operation. Said they'd quit if they couldn't choose us.  

I didn't change my pricing.  

The founder went with the cheaper option.  

The CFO actually quit.  

Six months later, the new CFO reached out. Asked if the pricing was still valid.  

They had chosen the underpriced option, spent six months trying to onboard, and learned a very, very expensive lesson.  

Price is not cost

article.title: Onchain Assets Weekly: 5/1/26
article.preview_text: This week’s highlights: Securitize partners with Computershare to tokenize U.S. stocks; Securitize COO joins The Rollup; BUIDL on OKX; Securitize VP on tokenization’s shift; and a tokenization

tweet.text:
Securitize partners with Computershare, the world's largest transfer agent, to bring US stocks onchain.

Listed companies can now issue tokenized shares alongside traditional ones - "actual shares in token form, not derivatives or wrappers." No changes to existing capital structure required.

Computershare manages shares for ~60% of the S&P 500 including Apple $AAPL, Tesla $TSLA, Microsoft $MSFT, Nvidia $NVDA, and Coinbase $COIN.

profile_bio.description: The Fastest Stock Market News Account on X
